articles.title: Nurse convicted in patient's death is now a national speaker on hospital safety
articles.summary: RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Russia uses hypersonic Oreshnik missile in mass attack on Kyiv
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Sunday Russia used the powerful hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile during a mass drone and missile attack on Kyiv on Sunday that killed at least two peo

articles.title: With Big Decisions Ahead, the Supreme Court Collides With a Testy Trump
articles.summary: President Trump has alternated between bullying the justices and cozying up to them as the court prepares to announce major decisions that will determine the fate of the key aspects of his agenda.

articles.title: Sanders and Ocasio-Cortez Try Boosting Progressives in Red Districts
articles.summary: In highly competitive House races that Democrats need to flip in November, the left is betting that calls for universal health care and taxing the wealthy will resonate — even among Republicans.

articles.title: RFK Jr.’s Push to Curb Antidepressants Has Shaken Psychiatry
articles.summary: An annual psychiatric meeting was abuzz over Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s call to rein in the use of depression medications. Some fear it will drive patients away from care.

articles.title: Trump Endorsement Shakes John Cornyn in Texas Senate Runoff Against Ken Paxton
articles.summary: A tough re-election race grew more daunting after President Trump backed Mr. Cornyn’s opponent, Ken Paxton. The Texas senator has vowed to fight to the end.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets and Crypto Firms Steamrolled a Watchdog Agency
articles.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission purged its ranks, dialed back its enforcement and boosted industries in which President Trump’s family is heavily invested.

articles.title: Do U.S. Automakers Have a Future in Canada?
articles.summary: Detroit-based automakers pioneered open trade between Canada and the United States, benefiting both countries. President Trump’s trade war now leaves their future in the country in question.

articles.title: 'Homeland Security' has spawned political insecurity since DHS was born
articles.summary: DHS was originally conceived in the interest of unity and harmony — and the phrase "homeland security" was originally meant to be reassuring.

articles.title: The Enhanced Games are Sunday. Here's what to know about the controversial event
articles.summary: Dozens of athletes — including former Olympians — will participate in the Las Vegas event while using performance-enhancing drugs.

articles.title: Torn by war, Israelis and Palestinians tie their fortunes together
articles.summary: At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.

articles.title: Trump Is Setting His Sights on Restricting Legal Immigration
articles.summary: After a year focused on immigrants here illegally, the Trump administration is now making it harder for legal migrants to stay in the country. It is a risky pivot.

articles.title: Gunman in Shooting Near White House Killed in Exchange of Fire With Secret Service
articles.summary: A gunman known to the U.S. Secret Service opened fire near the White House and was shot and killed by federal officers. President Trump was in the White House at the time.

articles.title: Suspect dead after opening fire near White House security checkpoint, Secret Service says
articles.summary: A man who opened fire Saturday near a White House security checkpoint is dead after being shot by officers who returned fire, the U.S. Secret Service said. It was the third incidence of gunfire in the

articles.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
articles.summary: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

articles.title: Trump touts Iran breakthrough but details remain unclear
articles.summary: President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. Iran did not officially confirm the deal, and state media contradicted parts of it.

articles.title: The squeeze on Cuba now includes compensation lawsuits
articles.summary: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in conte

articles.title: Severe global food crisis could come within the year, says UN agency
articles.summary: Maximo Torero, Chief Economist at The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, warns that the war in Iran is choking the global supply of fertilizer and a food crisis could follow with

articles.title: Video shows Israeli 'double-tap strike' killed medics and a toddler in Lebanon
articles.summary: In Lebanon, an Israeli 'double tap' killed three medics and four others including a two-year-old girl. A neighbor's video shows what happened.

articles.title: What will Trump do next with Iran?
articles.summary: Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.

articles.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
articles.summary: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
